items.body:
Founded in 2013 under International Wine & Beverage Company, CheckMate focuses on ultra premium Chardonnay and Merlot, showcasing the potential of the Okanagan with a strong sense of place. The estate leans into a refined, modern luxury style while staying true to its terroir, with a cohesive chess theme that reflects the precision of the wines.

On the Merlot side, the South Okanagan, particularly Osoyoos, provides the heat needed to build structure and depth, resulting in wines that feel more powerful than many Ontario expressions. The Chardonnays were the standout, especially the 2021 Little Pawn, which showed vibrant citrus, layered fruit, and impressive texture while remaining fresh and balanced.

Overall, the experience is elevated from start to finish, with thoughtful winemaking, a stunning property, and strong hospitality. It comes at a premium, but you are paying for more than just the wine, it is the full experience.
items.title: CheckMate Artisanal Winery

items.body:
Founded in 1993, Burrowing Owl Estate Winery is a well-established South Okanagan producer with a beautiful property and a warm, welcoming feel. The setting stands out, relaxed, scenic, and easy to enjoy.

That said, the wines didn’t fully deliver for me. Everything was refined, but I found them lacking depth and intensity compared to what the region can offer. The tasting room experience was also a bit flat and less engaging than others in the valley.

I have seen Burrowing Owl quite a bit in Ontario as well, which isn’t super common for Okanagan wineries and speaks to how established they are.

Where they really win is the food, easily the highlight. Great quality, well done, and paired with very reasonable pricing. Overall, while the wine and service didn’t stand out, the property, food, and value still make it worth a visit.
items.title: Burrowing Owl Estate Winery
